rule.desc: When using abstract base classes, use `metaclass=ABCMeta` with `@abstractmethod` decorator and ellipsis body.
rule.when_to_use: - Defining abstract base classes with methods subclasses must implement - Creating interfaces with semantic inheritance (use Protocol for structural typing) - Combining with `@property`, `@classmethod`, `@staticmethod` for various abstract members
rule.rationale: - `metaclass=ABCMeta` keeps MRO simpler than inheriting from `ABC` - `@abstractmethod` decorator provides compile-time checking that subclasses implement methods - Ellipsis (`...`) is the modern, clean way to indicate "no implementation" - `raise NotImplementedError` is runtime checking; `@abstractmethod` is compile-time
rule.tool_support: - mypy enforces `@abstractmethod` at type-check time - Python raises `TypeError` if abstract class is instantiated - `@abstractmethod` works with `@property`, `@classmethod`, `@staticmethod`
